title: 11 Herbal Creams For Emphysema
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your emphysema treatment plan. These creams often contain herbs that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease the symptoms of emphysema. One example is Eucalyptus globulus, a herb commonly used in cough suppressants and decongestants. When applied topically, it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe.  Another herb, Echinacea purpurea, is known for its ability to boost the immune system. When used in a cream, it can help the body to fight off infections that can exacerbate emphysema symptoms.  Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the airways and improve breathing.  Using herbal creams can bring many benefits to your life. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, you may find that you're able to breathe more easily and have more energy. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing shortness of breath or fatigue due to emphysema.  Herbal creams can also be a natural alternative to prescription medications, which can have side effects and interact with other medications. By using a cream that contains herbs, you may be able to reduce your reliance on prescription medications and improve your overall health.  It's worth noting that while herbal creams can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. Consult with your doctor before using any new creams or medications, especially if you're already taking prescription medications for emphysema.  When choosing a herbal cream, look for one that contains high-quality, organic ingredients and has been tested for purity and potency. This will help to ensure that you're getting the most benefit from the cream and minimizing any potential risks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains eucalyptol and camphor as its main bioactive constituents. Eucalyptol has decongestant properties that help ease congestion and coughing, a common symptom of emphysema. Camphor also has expectorant properties, which help bring up mucus from the lungs, making it easier to breathe. The anti-inflammatory properties of eucalyptol and camphor may also help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier for people with emphysema to breathe. These properties work together to help improve breathing and reduce symptoms associated with emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second intervals, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add distilled water to the melted mixture and stir well.
Add Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and stir well. Be careful not to add too much oil, as it can be overpowering.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides that provide anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and inflammation in the lungs, which can be beneficial for people with emphysema. The polysaccharides in Echinacea purpurea creams stimulate the production of macrophages, white blood cells that help fight off infections and clean the lungs. The alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative damage and repair damaged lung tissue. By reducing inflammation and protecting the lungs, Echinacea purpurea creams may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema, such as shortness of breath and wheezing.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers. Add the vitamin E oil and mix well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Echinacea purpurea cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid as its active constituents. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the lungs, a common issue for people with emphysema. Rosmarinic acid also has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax airway muscles and improve breathing. Carnosic acid has been shown to have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect lung tissue from damage. By reducing inflammation and improving lung function, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may provide relief from emphysema symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup coconut oil, 1/2 cup beeswax, 2 tablespoons olive oil, 2 tablespoons rosemary essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Combine melted mixture with olive oil and rosemary essential oil in a bowl. Stir well.
Pour mixture into small containers or a silicone mold. Let cool and solidify.
Once solid, apply a small amount of cream to the chest area 2-3 times a day to help relieve emphysema symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that help improve lung function. Terpenoids, specifically bilobalide and ginkgolides, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, are antioxidants that protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba help to relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe for people with emphysema. By reducing inflammation and protecting lung tissue, Ginkgo biloba creams may help slow the progression of emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the shea butter and Ginkgo biloba extract to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove from heat and stir in the vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool until solid.
Once solid, your Ginkgo biloba cream is ready to use. Store it in an airtight container in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains licorice extract, which has been used to help manage emphysema symptoms. The active constituents, glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Glycyrrhizin also helps to relax the airway muscles, allowing for more air to enter the lungs. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids help to protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and protecting lung tissue, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help to slow the progression of emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, and 2 teaspoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat. Stir until smooth.
Add glycerin and dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Add distilled water to the mixture and stir until combined. Remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains turmeric as its active ingredient, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of curcuminoids, specifically curcumin. Curcumin helps reduce inflammation in the lungs, a key factor in emphysema, and its antioxidant properties protect lung tissue from damage caused by free radicals. Curcuma longa also contains demethoxycurcumin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The combination of these compounds in Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs. The bioavailability of curcumin in Curcuma longa creams is also enhanced by the presence of piperine, a compound that increases the absorption of curcumin by up to 2,000%.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in the turmeric powder. Let it cool slightly.
Pour the mixture into a container and refrigerate until it's solid. Use the cooled cream as needed for emphysema symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs associated with emphysema. Ginger's ability to relax airway muscles and improve lung function can also help alleviate breathing difficulties. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce the production of mucus in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and improving lung function, Zingiber officinale creams may help alleviate symptoms of emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Dry the Zingiber officinale root in a low-temperature oven for 2 hours.
Grind the dried Zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground Zingiber officinale powder with 2 cups of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax melts and the mixture thickens into a cream. Let it cool before applying to affected areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is a common issue for people with emphysema. The antispasmodic properties of linalool can also help relax airway muscles and improve breathing. Additionally, the antioxidants in Lavandula angustifolia creams can help protect the lungs from oxidative damage and promote healthy cell function. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy lung function, Lavandula angustifolia creams may be beneficial for people with emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of lavender essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Stir in the lavender essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the lungs, which is common in emphysema. Thymol also has expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Carvacrol has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. By reducing inflammation and clearing mucus, Thymus vulgaris creams may help to alleviate some symptoms of emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine Thymus vulgaris leaves and coconut oil in a saucepan. Heat over low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture and discard the Thymus vulgaris leaves. Add beeswax, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. Heat until beeswax melts.
Remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes. Pour mixture into a cream base and whip until smooth.
Store the Thymus vulgaris cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Use as directed to help alleviate emphysema symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains quinine, an alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory properties. Quinine helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, which is a common issue in people with emphysema. The cream also contains cinchonine, another alkaloid that has bronchodilatory properties, which means it helps to widen the airways and improve breathing. The antispasmodic properties of cinchonine also help to reduce muscle spasms in the airways, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and improving airflow, Cinchona officinalis creams may help to alleviate symptoms of emphysema.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 10 drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis leaves to the melted oil and beeswax mixture and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the leaves.
Add the vitamin E oil and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains bioactive constituents like rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and damage in the lungs. Rosmarinic acid, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which can contribute to lung damage. Carnosic acid has antioxidant properties that can help protect lung cells from oxidative stress, which is a common factor in emphysema. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Salvia officinalis creams may help slow the progression of emphysema and improve lung function.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for emphysema
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, then add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves.
Let the mixture simmer for 2-3 hours, then strain it through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir until the beeswax is melted.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and harden before using it as a cream for emphysema relief.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent emphysema from forming by reducing inflammation and protecting the lungs. Some teas, like those containing ginger and turmeric, have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties could help keep the airways clear and strong, potentially reducing the risk of emphysema. Regular herbal tea consumption may be beneficial for lung health.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing addition to your emphysema treatment, but it's essential to consider the ingredients. Some teas, like thyme and licorice root, may help ease breathing, while others can irritate the lungs. Consuming them daily can be safe if you choose teas with gentle properties and avoid those that might worsen your condition.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas may help with emphysema symptoms, but it's hard to say exactly when they'll start working. Some people notice a slight difference in breathing after a few days or a week. Others might not feel much change until a few weeks or months of consistent use. Results can vary from person to person.
faq_time: For people with emphysema, drinking herbal tea in the morning can be helpful. This is because it can help loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Many herbal teas, such as thyme and eucalyptus, have decongestant properties that can aid in breathing.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas may interact with prescription medications for emphysema. Some herbal teas contain compounds that can affect how the body processes certain drugs, making them more or less effective. This interaction can be especially concerning for medications that thin mucus, making it easier to breathe. Drinking herbal teas while taking emphysema medication should be done with caution.
faq_pregnant: If you have emphysema and are pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas can affect the baby's growth or cause other problems. Peppermint and ginger teas might be safe, but others like chamomile and licorice root could be a concern due to their potential effects on blood pressure and hormones.
faq_breastfeeding: While breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas for emphysema. Some herbs, like comfrey, are not recommended because they can pass into breast milk and harm the baby. However, others like ginger and peppermint are generally considered safe in small amounts. Still, it's a good idea to check the ingredients and choose teas from reputable sources.
title_2: 11 Natural Ways to Combat Emphysema for Farmers Using Herbal Creams
